rows.modal: must / mustn't, have to, need to
rows.example: Students **must** bring their ID cards to the exam. / You **have to / need to** register before you can log in.

rows.modal: don't have to, don't need to, needn't
rows.example: You **don't have to / don't need to / needn't** print the ticket — they'll scan it on your phone.
We **had to** walk home because we missed the last bus.

rows.modal: didn't have to, didn't need to
rows.example: There was food at the hotel, so we **didn't have to / didn't need to** go to a restaurant.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: In everyday speech, **have to** is used more often than **must**. **Must** tends to appear in signs, rules and written instructions.
items: "I **have to** finish this before tomorrow," he said.
Passengers **must** keep their bags with them at all times.

content.subtitle: Be careful
content.description: **Mustn't** and **don't have to** carry very different meanings.

notes.correct: You **mustn't** open that door! (= It is forbidden — do not do it.)
You **don't have to** open that door. (= You have a choice — it is not necessary.)

rows.example: She's been working since dawn — she **must** be exhausted. / That **can't / couldn't** be right — I checked the answer twice.
The parcel **should / ought to** arrive by Thursday — it was sent two days ago.
I'm not sure where she is — she **could / may / might** be at the library.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: We typically use **must**, **can't** and **couldn't** when we have evidence for our conclusion.
items: His coat is here but he isn't. He **must** have stepped outside for a moment.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: To speculate about past events, use a modal followed by the perfect infinitive (have + past participle).

sections.title: A: Complete with must, have to, need to or mustn't
sections.instructions: Write the best modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... show your ticket before entering the stadium.
We ...... hurry or the shop will close.
I ...... send this form before midnight.
Guests ...... take food into the swimming pool area.
All players ...... wear the correct boots for the match.
You ...... open that file. It may contain a virus.
I ...... remember to lock the back door tonight.
Do we ...... arrive before eight?

sections.title: B: Choose don't have to, don't need to or needn't
sections.instructions: Complete each sentence with a form showing no obligation.

items.sentence: You ...... wash the dishes. I've already done them.
It's a bank holiday tomorrow, so Dad ...... go to work.
You ...... come with us if you're too tired.
We ...... bring chairs; the school will provide them.
Lily ...... study French this year because she chose German.
You ...... answer now. Think about it first.
I ...... get a taxi because my brother can drive me.
You ...... write your name twice on the form.

sections.title: C: Past obligation
sections.instructions: Complete using had to, didn't have to or didn't need to.

items.sentence: We ...... wait outside because the doors were locked.
I ...... take my umbrella because it didn't rain.
Alex ...... finish his homework before dinner yesterday.
They ...... wear sports clothes because the match was cancelled.
I ...... ask for directions; I had a map.
Clara ...... stay in hospital for two nights.
We ...... buy tickets. My uncle had already bought them.
Tom ...... get up early on Sunday, so he slept until ten.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct phrase
sections.instructions: Choose the correct option in each sentence.

items.sentence: You **mustn't / don't have to** come if you have other plans.
You **mustn't / don't have to** touch the paintings in the gallery.
We **had to / didn't have to** wait long because the bus arrived immediately.
I **need to / mustn't** buy a birthday present for my sister.
All visitors **must / needn't** wear a badge in this building.
Nina **had to / didn't have to** call a mechanic when her car stopped.
You **needn't / mustn't** explain it again — I understand.
We **don't have to / mustn't** lose these documents.

sections.title: E: Probability and possibility
sections.instructions: Complete with must, can't, couldn't, should, ought to, could, may or might.

items.sentence: That ...... be Omar. He always wears that red jacket.
This ...... be Anna's bag — she never carries a backpack.
The meeting ...... finish late if there are many questions.
Your parcel ...... arrive tomorrow morning.
I'm not sure where Dad is. He ...... be in the garage.
The shop is closed and the lights are off. It ...... be open.
The team has trained hard, so they ...... win the match.
Take a jumper — it ...... get cold later.

sections.title: F: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one modal verb mistake. Write the correct modal.

items.sentence: You look ill. You **must** see a doctor.
This **should** be Daniel's notebook. His name is on the cover.
We **must** bring lunch; food is provided at the camp.
You **needn't** touch those chemicals. They are dangerous.
She left an hour ago, so she **can't** arrive soon.
He **mustn't** work late yesterday because the deadline was today.

sections.title: G: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each sentence has one modal error. Write the correct form.

items.sentence: You don't have to cross the road when the light is red.
I must to finish this exercise today.
We had to not bring books to the trip.
That must be Sophie; she's on holiday in Portugal.
You needn't to pay for the museum today.
This may is the correct password, but I'm not certain.

sections.title: H: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given.

items.sentence:
It's necessary for passengers to fasten their seatbelts. **MUST**
Passengers ...... their seatbelts.

It wasn't necessary for me to phone the office. **HAVE**
I ...... to phone the office.

I'm almost sure that's Maya's umbrella. **MUST**
That ...... Maya's umbrella.

Maybe they'll visit us next weekend. **MIGHT**
They ...... us next weekend.

It's prohibited to enter this area. **MUSTN'T**
You ...... this area.

It's probable that the train will arrive soon. **SHOULD**
The train ...... soon.

sections.title: I: Mixed revision
sections.instructions: Write one suitable modal form in each gap.

items.sentence: You ...... bring a towel; the hotel provides one.
The film starts in ten minutes, so we ...... go now.
His phone is on the table, so he ...... be far away.
I ...... finish the poster today. The deadline is next week.
She practised a lot, so she ...... do well in the concert.
You ...... feed the animals in the park. It's forbidden.
The car is in the drive, so Dad ...... be home.
We ...... walk yesterday because Mum picked us up.
I'm not sure, but this key ...... open the side door.
The lift was broken, so we ...... use the stairs.

sections.title: J: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using a maximum of four words, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence.

items.sentence:
There is no need for you to bring your dictionary.
...... bring your dictionary.

The rules say you cannot eat in this room.
You ...... in this room.

I'm certain he is in the garden now.
He ...... in the garden.

It was necessary for every player to arrive early.
Every player ...... early.

Perhaps she missed the bus.
She ...... the bus.

You are not required to bring your own lunch.
...... bring your own lunch.
